mcqs.question: What is the main difference between a simple reflex agent and a model-based reflex agent?

options.text: Model-based reflex agents maintain an internal model
Simple reflex agents are more complex
Model-based reflex agents are less flexible

mcqs.explanation: Model-based reflex agents maintain an internal model of the environment, which allows them to make more informed decisions.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the controller in an agent architecture?

options.text: To make decisions and send commands to actuators

mcqs.explanation: The controller is the decision-making component that processes information from sensors and sends commands to actuators.

mcqs.question: What is the advantage of a goal-based agent?

options.text: It can plan and achieve specific goals

mcqs.explanation: Goal-based agents can plan and achieve specific goals, which makes them more effective in certain applications.

mcqs.question: What is the disadvantage of a utility-based agent?

options.text: It requires a utility function specification

mcqs.explanation: Utility-based agents require a utility function specification, which can be challenging to define and implement.

mcqs.question: What is the main difference between a model-based reflex agent and a utility-based agent?

options.text: Model-based reflex agents maintain an internal model
Utility-based agents make decisions based on a utility function
Model-based reflex agents are more flexible

mcqs.explanation: Utility-based agents make decisions based on a utility function, which estimates the desirability of each action.
